Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix lē negation display, add liaiina/listen vocab, fix a le false positives

- Remove 'a le' from WHOLE_PHRASES (was falsely glossing 2,307 genitive
  "of the" as "if not/unless" — e.g. "a le tupu" = "of the king" not
  "if not king")
- Fix verb substring matching in le negation handler: 'do' in 'kingdom'
  was True, 'rule' in 'ruler' was True. Now uses word-level matching.
- Smart lē display modernization: only applies macron when le is at chunk
  start with "not" gloss, or after te/TAM markers. Prevents false macrons
  on article "the". Count: 13,912 → 6,309.
- Add liaiina → "pluck up" (34 verses, was unknown)
- Update faalogo → "listen" (was archaic "hearken", 1,238 verses)
- Add faalogo mai / fa'alogo mai → "listen" WHOLE_PHRASES
- Add fetalai mai ai → "says" (prophetic formula)
- Add o loo fetalai mai ai → "says" (fixes "is said" bad grammar)
- Update faaumatia → "destroy" (was "destroyed", base form)
- Add listen, obey, pluck, teach, speak etc. to _VERB_GLOSSES
- Bump cache v123→v124

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace Error.isError() with instanceof Error checks (#277)

* fix: replace Error.isError with instanceof Error for browser compatibility

Error.isError is a TC39 proposal not yet available in all environments,
causing TypeError crashes on Chrome Mobile iOS and other older browsers.
Replaced all 10 occurrences across client and server files with
`instanceof Error` which is universally supported.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CzG9cv4nJtxWW9fsxzZYTa

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert backend changes

Fix websockets and other stuff (#51)

* feat: Display real-time vote counts on voting cards, enable party leaders to manually advance phases, and refresh movie picking on new recommendations.

* feat: Add swipe gesture support to MovieCard for mobile users

Swipe right to like, left to dislike, and down to skip with visual
overlay indicators and card transform feedback during the gesture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Stabilize WebSocket connection by storing callback props in refs

Callback identity changes were causing connect/disconnect cycles on every
WS message: message → setLastMessage → new context value → new
handleWsMessage → new onMessage prop → new connect → lifecycle re-runs.

Store onMessage/onConnect/onDisconnect in refs so connect only depends on
token. Wrap handleWsMessage in useCallback for additional stability.
Remove unused lastMessage state from usePartySocket (consumers use
context's lastMessage).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): map AppError subclasses to correct HTTP status codes instead of 500

All 7 web SDK modules (memories, spaces, relationships, ghost, profiles,
preferences) had local wrapError functions that mapped every caught
exception to kind 'internal' (HTTP 500). This meant validation errors,
not-found, and permission errors all returned 500.

Changes:
- Add shared wrapError() to src/web/errors.ts that checks instanceof AppError
  and preserves the error kind (validation->400, not_found->404, forbidden->403)
- Remove 7 duplicate local wrapError functions
- Replace ~25 plain throw new Error() in space.service.ts with typed
  ValidationError, NotFoundError, ForbiddenError from app-errors
- Add 5 new wrapError tests, update publish validation assertion

Version: 0.34.13

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Rename MONGO_URI → MONGODB_URI everywhere (centris_scraper, cleaner, sync)

feat: SEO enhancements and landing page conversion improvements

- Add 8 high-volume keywords (件名, 返信, 挨拶, 結び, English variants)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): serialize Android builds to prevent pnpm store corruption (#594)

Both Android workflows run on the same baci-android VPS runner. When
triggered simultaneously (e.g. workflow_dispatch), they both wipe and
recreate ~/setup-pnpm, corrupting each other's pnpm content-addressable
store and causing ENOENT errors during pnpm install.

Fix: use a shared concurrency group (baci-android-runner) with
cancel-in-progress: false so builds queue instead of racing.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(moderation): add diagnostic logging for API errors

The moderation service was silently swallowing Anthropic API errors,
making it impossible to debug 'Content moderation unavailable' in e1.
Now logs the HTTP status and response body on failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.3.1: fix observer heartbeat and spa_fallback response model

- Add 30s heartbeat to /observe SSE endpoint to prevent timeout disconnects
- Fix spa_fallback route with response_model=None (from __future__ annotations compat)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: stability, security, and input validation sweep across app.py

Critical bug:
- delete_tool: add missing return after 'currently lent out' flash so
  the tool is not silently deleted despite the guard

CSRF / session:
- Initialize CSRFProtect(app); exempt /api/tools (JSON API)
- Add PERMANENT_SESSION_LIFETIME=24h
- Use secrets.compare_digest() for OAuth state comparison

Input validation:
- add_tool, edit_tool: use .get('name') + non-empty check; wrap float()
  in try/except with user-friendly error message
- add_person, edit_person: same name validation

Error handling:
- return_tool: null-check the active loan row; flash instead of TypeError
- api_tools POST: remove force=True from get_json(); null-check fetchone()
- person_detail, tool_detail: return abort(404) instead of silent redirect
- Login config failure: return HTTP 500 instead of 200
- import_tools: wrap all inserts in a single transaction; rollback on any
  row error to prevent partial imports; catch UnicodeDecodeError explicitly
- edit_tool: delete old image file only after conn.commit() succeeds to
  avoid data/file inconsistency on DB failure

Image handling:
- Replace bare except Exception with (IOError, OSError, UnidentifiedImageError)
  in optimize_image(), delete_tool_image(), add_tool(), and edit_tool()

Logging:
- Replace all debug print() calls with app.logger.debug/info/warning/error
- /health endpoint now runs SELECT 1 to verify DB connectivity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add CSRF tokens to all POST forms and fix XSS in delete modals

- Add <input name="csrf_token"> to every HTML POST form:
  add_tool, add_person, edit_tool, edit_person, edit_loan, lend_tool,
  tool_detail (return form), user_settings (import form)
- Add <meta name="csrf-token"> to base.html so JS-created forms can

  as a hidden input before submit
- Fix XSS: replace '{{ tool.name }}' / '{{ person.name }}' string

  tool/person names containing single quotes or backslashes

fix: infrastructure hardening — non-root user, restart policy, pinned deps

commit.message:
fix: harden session cookies and guard SECRET_KEY in production

- Raise RuntimeError if SECRET_KEY is unset in production, preventing
  silent random key generation that invalidates all sessions on restart
- Set SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=True and SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE=Lax on
  all environments; SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True in production only
  (False in development to avoid breaking HTTP local dev)

commit.message:
Add selectable GPS stop picker to conflict resolution form

Instead of just using first/last nearby ping for time prefill, the
conflict resolution page now:
- Detects dwell clusters from all truck pings within 500m of property
- Shows them as selectable cards in the resolution form
- Pre-selects clusters within 150m (on-site)
- Updates start/end times from the combined range of selected stops
- Supports selecting multiple stops (e.g., crew visited front + back)

commit.message:
feat: auth layer, plugin registry, settings panel, welcome screen, context compaction

- Token-based auth: generateToken/validateToken with constant-time compare
- REST endpoints check Authorization header or ?token= query param
- POST /api/auth/verify for web UI token validation
- GET /api/health with uptime, plugin counts, session count
- Plugin registry (persistent ~/.bogs/plugins.json) tracks all plugins ever connected
- GET /api/registry returns active + historical plugins
- bogs init now prompts for access token (auto-generate or custom)
- bogs token show/reset/clear commands
- Conversation compaction: summarizes and drops old messages when history exceeds 80k chars
- Web UI: AuthScreen with token entry when server has auth enabled
- Web UI: SettingsPanel (model, API key, clear sessions) behind gear icon
- Web UI: WelcomeScreen with suggestion chips on empty sessions
- Web UI: PluginBar registry dropdown with connection history
- All fetch calls pass Authorization header when token is set

commit.message:
fix: add HTTP timeouts and error handling to OIDC auth

  OIDC provider no longer hangs the login flow indefinitely
- Fix resp.text reference bug in get_token(): use e.response.text
  instead of bare resp.text, which was unbound on connection errors
- Catch RequestException broadly in both methods and log before re-raising

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use longest dwell cluster for conflict resolution time prefill

Previously the start/end times were prefilled from the first/last nearby
ping within 150m of the property for the entire day. A truck passing by
later (e.g. heading home) would stretch the end time far past the actual
service window. Now the JS dwell cluster detection (which groups
consecutive stationary pings) updates the form fields with the longest
cluster's time range — the "likely service" window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix booking API: use update for existing bookings, trim APP_URL newline

- Root cause: NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL env var has trailing \n, causing Stripe
  to reject checkout session URLs as invalid
- Added .trim() to appUrl to handle whitespace/newlines in env vars
- Replaced delete+insert with update for existing pending bookings
  (RLS has no DELETE policy on bookings table)
- Added fallback to request.url origin if APP_URL is missing
- Better error messages showing actual failure reason
